description: Implement the Gua-Ci Yu-Yi (Hexagram Text Significance) system for the changed hexagram (bian-gua). According to the theory, the bian-gua's traditional hexagram name/text carries guidance meaning - it serves as 'half a guidance hexagram'. This includes: Liu-He/Liu-Chong hexagram detection with their specific implications, hexagram name symbolism interpretation, and the guidance function of certain bian-gua patterns.
steps: Create liuyao/guaci.py with: (a) A comprehensive GUA_CI_YUYI dict mapping each of the 64 hexagram names to their symbolic meanings. Include at minimum the key hexagrams mentioned in the knowledge base: {name: 'hexagram_name', keywords: [keywords], guidance: 'guidance text', polarity: 'positive/negative/neutral', domain_hints: {domain: interpretation}}. Cover all hexagrams referenced: Qian (beginning/strength), Kun (acceptance/nurturing), Shi (pursue/investigate), Xu (wait/observe), Guan (observe/watch), Jia-Ren (protect wealth/family), Tai (smooth/unobstructed), Pi (blocked/obstructed), Jie (restriction/moderation), Kun-hexagram (trapped/restricted), Bi (adornment/ceremony), Lu (travel/movement), Fu (return/repeat), Wu-Wang (no harm/harmless), Da-Zhuang (peak/maturity), Yu (hesitation/pleasure), Jian (gradual/steady), Heng (constant/enduring), etc. (b) A function get_guaci_interpretation(bian_gua_name, question_type) that returns the relevant interpretation for the changed hexagram in context of the question type.

Implement Liu-Chong-Gua (Six Clash Hexagram) analysis: function analyze_liuchong_gua(ben_gua_name, bian_gua_name) that: (a) Detects if ben-gua or bian-gua is a liu-chong hexagram (the 6 pure-clash hexagrams: Qian, Kun, Kan, Li, Zhen-bianliu-chong patterns where all lines clash). (b) Detects the specific six-chong hexagram identities (Qian=starting/rigid, Kun=bearing/passive, Li=beautiful/revealed, Xun=faithful/following, Zhen=movement/travel, Kan=trapped/concave, Gen=blocked/piled, Dui=speech/dispute). (c) Returns implications: for short-term questions (quick/changeable/not lasting), for long-term questions (unstable/cannot endure). (d) Implements the special liu-chong-bian-liu-he and liu-he-bian-liu-chong patterns from the knowledge base (clash->harmony = recovery from setback; harmony->clash = current togetherness will break apart).

Implement Liu-He-Gua (Six Harmony Hexagram) analysis: function analyze_liuhe_gua(ben_gua_name, bian_gua_name) that: (a) Detects six-harmony hexagrams. (b) Returns implications: entanglement/delay for short-term, persistence/longevity for long-term. (c) Handles Pi (blocked), Kun (trapped), Jie (restricted) as harmony hexagrams with negative undertones, and Tai as harmony with positive undertones.

Implement bian-gua guidance function: function get_bian_gua_guidance(hexagram, jixiong_result, dongbian_results) that: (a) When jixiong is unfavorable AND bian-gua is liu-he, suggests 'pause/defer action'. (b) When jixiong is unfavorable AND bian-gua is liu-chong, suggests 'abort/stop pursuit'. (c) When bian-gua name carries specific guidance keywords (Shi=investigate, Xu=wait, Guan=observe, Jia-Ren=conserve), outputs that guidance. (d) Notes that guidance function only activates when core dynamic information shows unfavorable trend; favorable hexagrams use bian-gua for objective description not guidance.

Add detection of Fan-Yin (反吟, counter-groan) and Fu-Yin (伏吟, hidden-groan) at gua level: function detect_gua_fanyin_fuyin(hexagram) that checks if ben-gua and bian-gua form a clash pair (fan-yin: e.g., Zhen<->Dui) or same-palace pattern (fu-yin). Returns type and implications (fan-yin = reversal/repetition, fu-yin = stagnation/complaint).

Integrate guaci module into analyzer.py: After jixiong judgment, call guaci analysis for the bian-gua. Store in report.guaci_result. Also detect and store liu-chong/liu-he status for both ben and bian gua.
Update report.py to include '【卦辞寓意】' section showing: bian-gua name interpretation, liu-chong/liu-he status and implications, guidance message if applicable, fan-yin/fu-yin detection.

Write tests in tests/test_guaci.py: (a) Test GUA_CI_YUYI coverage for key hexagrams, (b) Test liu-chong detection for known six-clash hexagrams, (c) Test liu-he detection, (d) Test liu-chong-bian-liu-he special pattern interpretation, (e) Test guidance function activation conditions, (f) Test fan-yin/fu-yin detection, (g) Test integration with full pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: python3 -m pytest tests/test_guaci.py -v passes all tests
get_guaci_interpretation('水天需', 'cai') returns interpretation containing 'wait/observe' guidance
analyze_liuchong_gua correctly identifies Qian-wei-Tian as a liu-chong hexagram
Liu-chong-bian-liu-he pattern ('天风姤' -> some liu-he) returns 'recovery from setback' interpretation
Guidance function activates when jixiong='凶' and bian-gua is liu-he, suggesting pause
verification: Run python3 -m pytest tests/ -v to confirm no regressions
findings: All 56 new tests pass. All 317 total tests pass (261 pre-existing + 56 new). Liu-he detection uses actual na-jia di-zhi pairs (positions 1-4, 2-5, 3-6). Confirmed di-tian-tai and tian-di-pi are both liu-he hexagrams. End-to-end verification with --date 2024-01-15 shows guaci section in report output.
